hero.headline: State income tax and unemployment insurance in New Jersey
hero.subhead: Five payroll taxes, one $44,800 wage base, a top income rate of 10.75%. Here's what you owe in 2026.

income_tax.question: What is the New Jersey state income tax rate for employers in 2026?
income_tax.answer_first: New Jersey state income tax uses seven graduated brackets in 2026, from 1.4% on the first $20,000 to 10.75% above $1,000,000. Employers withhold using NJ-W4 elections.

payroll_stack.question: What is the full New Jersey employer payroll tax stack in 2026?
payroll_stack.answer_first: New Jersey employers pay UI, TDI, and WF/SWF on the $44,800 wage base. Employees contribute UI, TDI, FLI, and WF/SWF — with TDI and FLI using the higher $171,100 wage base.

ui_tdi_fli.question: What are the New Jersey UI, TDI, and FLI rates for 2026?
ui_tdi_fli.answer_first: UI: $44,800 wage base, new employers pay 2.8% combined (about $1,254/employee/year). TDI maximum weekly benefit is $1,119. FLI employee rate is 0.23% on $171,100.

family_leave.question: What family leave does New Jersey require employers to provide in 2026?
family_leave.answer_first: The New Jersey Family Leave Act (NJFLA) provides 12 weeks of unpaid, job-protected leave per 24-month period. From 17th July 2026, it covers employers with 15 or more employees — reduced from 30.
family_leave.earned_sick_leave: 1 hour per 30 hours worked, max 40 hours/year, all employers

worker_classification.question: How does New Jersey classify workers as employees or independent contractors?
worker_classification.answer_first: New Jersey uses the ABC test under N.J.S.A. 43:21-19(i)(6). All three prongs must be satisfied to classify a worker as an independent contractor — the burden of proof is on the employer.

prongs.A: Free from control in fact and by contract
prongs.B: Work outside usual course of business or outside all places of business
prongs.C: Customarily engaged in independently established trade

worker_classification.regulatory_update: Final rules adopted 5 May 2026; operative 1 October 2026

warn.question: What does New Jersey's WARN Act require for mass layoffs?
warn.answer_first: New Jersey's WARN Act requires 90 days notice plus one week of severance per year of service for qualifying mass layoffs. It covers employers with 100 or more employees anywhere in the US.
warn.official_name: Millville Dallas Airmotive Plant Job Loss Notification Act

good_to_know.question: What should out-of-state companies know before hiring in New Jersey?
good_to_know.answer_first: New Jersey's payroll layer is one of the most complex in the northeast. The two-tier wage base, July 2026 NJFLA expansion, and October 2026 ABC test rules mean three compliance deadlines in one calendar year.
good_to_know.body: Three things that catch out-of-state employers in New Jersey: the two-tier wage base, the July 2026 NJFLA expansion to 15 employees, and the October 2026 ABC test regulatory operative date.

internal_links.anchor: Hire in the United States: overview

faq_schema.q: What is the New Jersey UI tax rate for new employers in 2026?
faq_schema.a: New Jersey UI tax rate for new employers in 2026 is 2.8% combined (2.6825% UI plus 0.1175% WF/SWF) on the first $44,800 of each employee's wages. Experience-rated employers pay 0.5% to 3.6% under Tax Table C for positive reserve ratios.

faq_schema.q: What is New Jersey's state income tax top rate in 2026?
faq_schema.a: New Jersey's top state income tax rate is 10.75% in 2026, applying to taxable income above $1,000,000. The state uses seven graduated brackets from 1.4% on the first $20,000 up to 10.75% above $1 million.

faq_schema.q: Does New Jersey have Family Leave Insurance for employees?
faq_schema.a: Yes. New Jersey Family Leave Insurance (FLI) is funded entirely by employee payroll deductions at 0.23% on wages up to $171,100 in 2026 (maximum $393.53/year). Employees receive 85% of average weekly wages up to $1,119/week for up to 12 weeks of qualifying family leave.

faq_schema.q: What is the New Jersey WARN Act notice period?
faq_schema.a: New Jersey's WARN Act (Millville Dallas Airmotive Plant Job Loss Notification Act) requires 90 days advance notice for qualifying mass layoffs. Employers with 100 or more employees anywhere in the US must also pay one week of severance per year of service.

faq_schema.q: What is the New Jersey ABC test for independent contractors?
faq_schema.a: New Jersey uses the ABC test under N.J.S.A. 43:21-19(i)(6) to determine worker classification. Employers must establish all three prongs: free from control, work outside usual business, independently established trade. NJDOL adopted final clarifying regulations on 5th May 2026, operative 1st October 2026.

meta.title: New Jersey State Income Tax and Unemployment Insurance 2026 | Teamed
meta.description: New Jersey payroll taxes 2026: UI 2.8% new employer on $44,800 wage base, TDI, FLI 0.23%, graduated income tax to 10.75%. Five-tax stack explained for employers.
